desc: The age of the individual in years.
The employment sector or status of the individual (e.g., Private, Self-employed, Government).
Final weight; a demographic weighting variable representing the estimated number of people in the population that this specific record represents.
The highest categorical level of education completed by the individual (e.g., Bachelors, HS-grad, Masters).
The highest level of education completed, represented as a continuous numerical value to indicate total years of schooling.
The current marital situation of the individual (e.g., Married-civ-spouse, Divorced, Never-married).
The specific type of work, profession, or trade the individual is engaged in (e.g., Exec-managerial, Craft-repair).
The individual's familial relationship to the primary householder in their living unit (e.g., Husband, Wife, Own-child, Not-in-family).
The reported race or ethnic categorization of the individual.
The biological sex of the individual (Female or Male).
Financial income generated from investment sources (outside of standard wage earnings), recorded as continuous capital gains.
Financial losses incurred from investment sources, recorded as continuous capital losses.
The typical number of hours the individual works per week.
The individual's primary country of origin or birth.
The target variable for classification; indicates whether the individual's annual income exceeds $50,000 (>50K) or is less than or equal to $50,000 (<=50K).
